description: Direction and robustness constraints for pheromone persistence, sensing and diffusion. These are literature-guided screens, not fitted physical half-lives or concentration units.

sources.title: Individual rules for trail pattern formation in Argentine ants (Linepithema humile)
sources.used_for: Local pheromone response should be a robust individual steering rule; extreme field settings should not be required to make trails work.

sources.title: A stochastic model of ant trail following with two pheromones
sources.used_for: Diffusion properties can alter collective movement patterns, so sensitivity to diffusion must be reported and bounded.

sources.title: A continuous model of ant foraging with pheromones and trail formation
sources.used_for: Trail formation and resource removal are coupled; pheromone persistence should support efficient foraging without preventing adaptation.

constraints.meaning: A calibrated persistent trail should not suppress food trips by more than 20% under heat/dry stress.
A calibrated persistent food trail should not materially amplify brood stress in heat/dry stress.
A calibrated persistent trail should not suppress resource-stress food trips by more than 20%.
Higher diffusion can change recruitment, but should stay inside a local robustness band before numeric curve fitting.
Higher evaporation and threshold should reduce peak food pheromone.
Higher brood demand may trade off with foraging, but should not collapse heat/dry food trips in this screen.

next_required_upgrade: Replace these qualitative bands with digitized literature curves for trail decay, trail choice, recruitment strength and brood survival.
